instruction: Switch to user charles (password Ex@mpleP@55w0rd!) without logging out, and as charles create /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt containing exactly: whoami=charles and home=/home/charles on separate lines. Then return to your original user session.

parameters.command: echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S bash -c 'id -u charles >/dev/null 2>&1 || useradd -m -s /bin/bash charles; echo "charles:Ex@mpleP@55w0rd!" | chpasswd; rm -f /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt'

result.command:
ok=1; [ -f /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt ] || ok=0; expected=$(cat <<'EOF'
whoami=charles
home=/home/charles
EOF
); actual=$(cat /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt 2>/dev/null); [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] || ok=0; [ "$(stat -c '%U' /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt 2>/dev/null)" = "charles" ] || ok=0; if [ "$ok" = "1" ]; then echo PASS; else echo FAIL; fi
